req.title: System-Admin deletes onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a onboarding-task through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers DELETE of an onboarding-task via REST API with role-based access control, mandatory field validation, and audit trail. I designed: (1) a positive EP test verifying a valid admin delete yields 200/201, entity id in response, and an audit record; (2) a NEG test verifying 401 when no auth token is supplied; (3) a NEG/DT test verifying 403 when a non-admin role attempts deletion; (4) a BVA test exercising deletion of a task whose id is at the minimum valid boundary (e.g. id=1); (5) a STC test confirming that a second delete of the same resource yields 404, proving the state transition from 'exists' to 'deleted' is correctly enforced and idempotent from the server perspective.

tests.name: test_admin_delete_onboarding_task_success
preconditions: A valid onboarding-task exists with known id
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token> fixture
Create a disposable onboarding-task via <create_onboarding_task> helper to get a fresh id
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Audit record for the deletion exists via <get_audit_record> helper
trace: R-WEB-027: positive path - authenticated admin deletes existing task
R-WEB-027: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_delete_onboarding_task_success(admin_token, base_url, create_onboarding_task, get_audit_record):
    task_id = create_onboarding_task(admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert str(task_id) in str(body), "Deleted entity id not present in response body"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=task_id, action="delete")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record was not written for the delete operation"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_onboarding_task_returns_401
preconditions: A valid onboarding-task id is known
actions: Send DELETE /api/onboarding-tasks/{id} without any Authorization header
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_delete_onboarding_task_returns_401(admin_token, base_url, create_onboarding_task):
    task_id = create_onboarding_task(admin_token)

    response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}",
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_onboarding_task_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'viewer' (non-admin)
actions: Obtain viewer auth token via <get_viewer_token> fixture
Send DELETE /api/onboarding-tasks/{id} with viewer Authorization header
trace: R-WEB-027: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_delete_onboarding_task_min_boundary_id
preconditions: An onboarding-task with the minimum valid integer id (id=1) exists or is seeded
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token> fixture
Seed or confirm onboarding-task with id=1 exists via <seed_min_task> helper
trace: R-WEB-027: boundary - minimum valid resource id processed correctly
R-WEB-027: entity deleted and confirmed absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_delete_onboarding_task_min_boundary_id(admin_token, base_url, seed_min_task):
    min_id = seed_min_task(admin_token, desired_id=1)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}

    del_response = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{min_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert del_response.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {del_response.status_code}: {del_response.text}"
    )
    body = del_response.json()
    assert str(min_id) in str(body), "Min boundary id not returned in delete response"

    get_response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{min_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert get_response.status_code == 404, (
        f"Expected 404 after deletion, got {get_response.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_double_delete_onboarding_task_returns_404
preconditions: An onboarding-task exists and has been deleted once (state: deleted)
actions: Obtain admin auth token via <get_admin_token> fixture
Create onboarding-task, delete it once successfully
Send DELETE /api/onboarding-tasks/{id} a second time with same admin token
expected: State transition from 'exists' -> 'deleted' is enforced; re-deletion is rejected
trace: R-WEB-027: state transition - resource cannot be deleted twice
R-WEB-027: idempotency boundary on DELETE verb
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_double_delete_onboarding_task_returns_404(admin_token, base_url, create_onboarding_task):
    task_id = create_onboarding_task(admin_token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    url = f"{base_url}/api/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}"

    first = requests.delete(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"First delete expected 200/201, got {first.status_code}: {first.text}"
    )

    second = requests.delete(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    assert second.status_code == 404, (
        f"Second delete expected 404, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    )
